summary: Session 2: the Explorer module tier (module -> status-bucket -> row). Protocol: ModulePayload { slug; title; buckets } + SnapshotPayload.modules replacing the top-level buckets field per the routed rendering ruling (Q2 - host and webview ship together; a duplicated field only invites divergence); RowPayload.slug and every action message unchanged (module is grouping, never identity). View-model: pure groupByModule(all) ordered by the new SessionSet.moduleOrder (the manifest entry index, stamped at scan time next to module/moduleTitle - ruling Q3; the small types.ts/fileSystem.ts touches beyond the spec's declared list are named in s2-conventions.md). Host: buildModules wraps the unchanged buildBuckets/buildRow. Webview: the single-implicit case renders BYTE-IDENTICAL pre-087 markup (proven by an old-vs-new innerHTML diff harness, 3136 bytes equal, incl. empty + cancelled buckets); multi-module workspaces render collapsible module groups in manifest file order with the implicit '(ungrouped)' group last (ruling Q1), aria-level 1 (module header) / 2 (bucket header) / 3 (row), per-module collapse state, and composite '<module>/<bucket>' collapse keys (ruling Q4). Ruling saved raw at s2-explorer-render-architecture.json (anthropic excluded after a read-timeout + a 400 on the architecture call; exclusion logged). Operator answered the requiresUAT/requiresE2E 'suggested' prompt with 'e2e' (suggestion_disposition entry): the Playwright gate is armed, no UAT checklist owed. Evidence: unit suite 1306 passing (13 new Layer-2 tests in moduleTier.test.ts), tsc + esbuild clean, eslint 7 pre-existing errors only (zero new), Layer 1 pytest 2922 passed / 6 skipped / 0 failed. Layer 3: first push d089272 (run 29123889271) was green on every job except the NEW smoke on windows-latest - a test-helper EOL bug (\n-only match vs the harness's CRLF specs), fixed newline-agnostic in 4e5516f; the fix commit's fully green run 29124453680 (Playwright Layer 3 green on all three OSes incl. the new smoke) is the Layer-3 / E2E-gate evidence of record. Local Electron launch remains unavailable in the agent shell (S1-documented limitation; six untouched CI-green specs fail at app.firstWindow locally - the environment control).

reason.specifics: Routed analysis (s2-next-orchestrator-analysis.json, gemini-pro) recommends an Opus-class Claude seat at medium effort for Session 3's mix (precise YAML/MD/TS file work + CODEOWNERS/monorepo-CI template authoring; no new rendering layer). Supervision points: the templates' path-scoping and ownership mappings, the scaffold action's typing across the webview action union + host handler, and error handling on the modules.yaml / project-plan.md writes.

deferred.item: Explorer UI affordance for duplicateNameError (row badge/tooltip + at most one throttled notification)
deferred.reason: Anticipated by the S1 collision ruling ('Session 2 is expected to render...') but absent from the operator-approved spec's Session 2 plan, which scopes S2 to the module tier only. Surfaced here per the scope-doubt rule instead of unilaterally expanding scope; the fail-loud data-layer behavior (flagged winner row + deduped console.error) shipped in S1 and is live. Candidate for Session 3/4 spare capacity or the 088 follow-on set.
